parts.text:
Blocking issue found.

- `handleCreateWorkspaceDeployment` still has multiple early returns that hardcode `200` for reused/concurrent deployments without checking terminal failure status: `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:426`, `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:474`, `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:522`, `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:546`. In provider precheck contention/race paths, if the concurrent deployment is already `failed`, this endpoint will still return `200` instead of `400`, so the replay semantics are not fully fixed.

- Associated API tests don’t cover this race path. The new failed-reuse test in `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.test.ts:428` exercises the simulated-provider path, not the Cloudflare precheck concurrency branches above, so it won’t catch the remaining `200`-on-failed behavior.

I also ran the worker test suite (`npm test` in `packages/worker`); it passes, but this specific edge case remains uncovered.
